questions.question: How can you create a new database from scratch?

options.text: Select "Build with AI" in the Get started menu.
options.explanation: Build with AI is used for generating content based on descriptions, not the initial creation of a blank table.

options.text: Use the slash command /database.
options.explanation: This is the standard way to create a new database on any page.

options.text: Create a new page and select "Database" under Get started.
options.explanation: Selecting 'Database' under Get started opens a template, not a blank table.

options.text: Go to the Settings tab and enable 'New Database'.
options.explanation: There is no global toggle in Settings for creating databases; you use the menu or slash commands.

questions.question: In a Notion table, what is the relationship between columns and properties?

options.text: Columns are for visual organization, while properties store data.
options.explanation: In Notion, columns and properties are essentially the same thing; each column represents a property.

options.text: Columns correspond to properties that give your item context.
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that each column corresponds to a property.

options.text: You cannot change the type of a column once it is created.
options.explanation: You can click a property name, select 'Edit property', and change the type to see all options.

options.text: Properties are only for sorting data, while columns are for editing.
options.explanation: Properties store the data and define the type of information (like text, dates, or select options).

questions.question: How do you create a database using Notion AI from the Home screen?

options.text: Use the slash command /database.
options.explanation: The slash command is used for standard databases, not the AI Build mode in Home.

options.text: Toggle to Build mode in the Notion AI widget.
options.explanation: This is the specific instruction for creating a database via AI from the Home screen.

options.text: Select one of the Suggested templates.
options.explanation: This method is used for creating databases from existing templates, not using AI generation.

options.text: Click 'Build with AI' in the menu that appears.
options.explanation: 'Build with AI' is the option available when you select 'Table' under Get started on a specific page.

questions.question: Which of the following actions is NOT supported when creating a database with Notion AI?

options.text: Creating a new database.
options.explanation: Notion AI can definitely create new databases using the Build mode.

options.text: Editing existing databases.
options.explanation: According to the Note, Notion AI can create new databases but cannot edit existing ones.

options.text: Creating database page templates.
options.explanation: The article notes that Notion AI cannot create database page templates.

options.text: Adding properties like priority or status.
options.explanation: Notion AI can generate databases with specific properties like priority levels and status.

questions.question: How do you add specific options (e.g., P1, P2) to a 'Priority' property?

options.text: Type the options manually into the cell.
options.explanation: While you can type, the article describes a specific menu method to ensure the property behaves as a selection field.

options.text: Select Add property and type the options manually.
options.explanation: You should select Add an option in the menu that appears after editing the property.

options.text: Click on the property name, select Edit property, and select Add an option.
options.explanation: This is the correct workflow described in the article to populate the 'Select' property type.

options.text: Go to Settings and change the property type to 'Select'.
options.explanation: The property type is already 'Select'; you just need to add options to the list.

questions.question: What happens when you open a database row as a page?

options.text: You can only edit the properties of that row.
options.explanation: You gain full access to the body of the page, allowing for rich content addition.

options.text: You can add or edit any properties and add additional content into the body of the page.
options.explanation: The article states that opening a row as a page allows for editing properties and adding content inside.

options.text: The row becomes read-only.
options.explanation: Opening a row as a page gives you full editing capabilities, similar to a standard page.

options.text: It closes the database view entirely.
options.explanation: It simply opens a new view of that specific item as a page within the database.

questions.question: Which view type is best suited if you are storing images in your database?

options.text: Timeline view.
options.explanation: Timeline is useful for visualizing project dates, not specifically for displaying images.

options.text: List view.
options.explanation: List view is best for minimalist storage of documentation, not necessarily showcasing images.

options.text: Gallery view.
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that Gallery view is great if you're storing images.

options.text: Calendar view.
options.explanation: Calendar view displays pages by an assigned date property, not by images.

questions.question: How do you filter or sort your data?

options.text: Select the database name and click 'Filter' in the sidebar.
options.explanation: You must select the settings menu at the top of the database to access Filter or Sort options.

options.text: Select the settings menu at the top of your database → Filter or Sort.
options.explanation: This is the exact instruction provided in the article to manage data visibility.

options.text: Use the slash command /filter.
options.explanation: There is no slash command for filtering; it is found in the database settings menu.

options.text: Filters apply automatically to the entire workspace.
options.explanation: Filters are specific to the view you are currently looking at.

questions.question: If you create a database using Build mode in Home, where is it added?

options.text: It is added to the specific page you are currently viewing.
options.explanation: The article notes that databases created in Home are added as a new page in the workspace, not attached to the current view.

options.text: It is added directly to the Notion AI widget.
options.explanation: The AI widget is used for creation, but the resulting database is a standalone page.

options.text: It is added to your workspace as a new page.
options.explanation: The Note section explicitly states that databases created with Build mode in Home will be added to your workspace as a new page.

options.text: It is added to the 'Build' mode menu.
options.explanation: The result is a functional database page, not a menu item.

questions.question: What does the '+' button next to the database name allow you to do?

options.text: Add a new property column.
options.explanation: The '+' button to the right of a property is used to add a new property. The '+' next to the database name is for creating views.

options.text: Create a new view of the data (e.g., Board, Calendar).
options.explanation: Clicking this menu allows you to select different visualizations like Board, Calendar, or Gallery.

options.text: Rename the database.
options.explanation: Renaming is done by clicking the database name itself in the sidebar.

options.text: Delete the entire database.
options.explanation: Deleting requires right-clicking or going to the database menu; the '+' button is specifically for views.

questions.question: What happens when you select the Date property type for a 'Due date' property?

options.text: The property disappears from the table.
options.explanation: The property remains and allows for date-specific data entry.

options.text: A date picker appears when you click on a cell.
options.explanation: The article states that clicking on a cell under a Date property shows the date picker.

options.text: It sorts automatically by year.
options.explanation: Sorting is manual; you must go to the Sort settings to organize by date.

options.text: You cannot add data to it.
options.explanation: You can add data, and it will appear in a date picker for easy selection.

questions.question: Which view is useful for visualizing project dates and length?

options.text: List view.
options.explanation: List view is for minimalist documentation storage.

options.text: Board view.
options.explanation: Board view is for grouping by status, not necessarily project timeline length.

options.text: Timeline view.
options.explanation: The article describes Timeline as useful for visualizing project dates and length.

options.text: Calendar view.
options.explanation: Calendar view displays pages by an assigned date property, which is useful for scheduling, not necessarily project length visualization.
